title: VETERANS SERVICES - 211info
text: HARNEY COUNTY VETERANS SERVICE OFFICE VETERANS SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF County Veterans Service Offices (CVSOs) serve as the entry points to understanding the benefits, resources, and entitlements available to veterans, their dependents, and survivors. CVSOs can provide the following: -Veterans' benefits counseling and claims preparation assistance, as well as advocacy throughout the claims process -Counseling on federal VA benefits and other state and federal ancillary benefits -Educational benefit claims assistance -Assistance for veterans who wish to enroll in health care -Assistance for requesting military service documents -Service-connected and non-service-related disability and survivor benefits -Burial benefits -Information about veteran home loans -Oregon Veterans' Home Conservatorship services, including total estate management for eligible individuals who are legally determined to be "protected persons" under Oregon law 17 S Alder Street Burns, OR 97720 Eligibility: Veterans with any discharge status other than dishonorable and their immediate family members (spouses, surviving spouses who have not remarried, children, or stepchildren) Hours: Walk in: Thursday 9am-noon Appointments only: Monday-Wednesday 9am-noon and 1pm-4pm Email (541) 573-1342 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Main phone (541) 573-1342 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Walk in or call for appointment FEES: None DOCUMENTS: DD214 AREA SERVED: Harney County Services: Benefits and Services Assistance * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Burial Benefits * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues General Health Insurance Information/Counseling * Veteran/Military Personnel Issues Military Records Other Services Offered At This Location: TEMPORARY FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE - OREGON VETERANS EMERGENCY FINANCIAL ASSISTANCE PROGRAM If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. HARNEY COUNTY VETERANS SERVICE OFFICE
